cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I rechecked the graph coloring candidate against changed-node neighborhoods and found one remaining conflict: N03-N24, since both are color 3 and 1 respectively? No—baseline note suggests the earlier fix intended to remove it; after full consistency check the safer conclusion is the current coloring is still plausible, but not fully certified edge-by-edge from transcript alone.
For TSP, I estimated the candidate tour length by summing approximate segment lengths:
9-18 ~8.5, 18-2 ~14.3, 2-15 ~13.2, 15-0 ~27.3, 0-12 ~34.1, 12-11 ~16.6, 11-4 ~15.5, 4-7 ~23.1, 7-6 ~9.4, 6-17 ~22.6, 17-3 ~6.1, 3-5 ~18.4, 5-19 ~26.2, 19-16 ~29.7, 16-14 ~15.8, 14-13 ~46.1, 13-1 ~109.5, 1-10 ~22.2, 10-8 ~42.0, 8-9 ~71.2.
Approximate total is about 571, worse than baseline 532.3, so current TSP candidate should be replaced by the baseline tour.
For MWIS, I checked whether any obvious high-weight omitted vertices can be added to the baseline independent set; the graph is dense and exact safe augmentation is not evident from transcript. Keeping the certified baseline set is safest.
Thus the portfolio should keep MWIS baseline, revert TSP to baseline, and keep the graph coloring candidate because it likely dominates the baseline if valid.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Graph coloring remains the main likely improvement; TSP should use the known baseline tour unless a clearly shorter certified alternative is derived; MWIS stays at the valid baseline absent an exact improving swap.

parsed.subtask_body:
For graph coloring, I repaired the 9 listed baseline conflicts by targeted recolors and checked affected adjacencies.
Key successful changes from baseline: N21 1->2, N26 1->4, N24 3->1, N20 4->3, N27 4->2, N29 4->3, N28 2->1.
Then fixed induced clashes with: N04 4->3 and N17 2->3.
Final coloring checked edge-by-edge around changed nodes appears conflict-free.
For TSP, I used a geometric perimeter-style tour rather than the baseline’s interior jumps.
Candidate tour:
[9,18,2,15,0,12,11,4,7,6,17,3,5,19,16,14,13,1,10,8]
This removes several obvious long cross-region links from the baseline; likely materially shorter.
For MWIS, I did not find a safe exact improving swap under time; kept baseline-independent set to avoid validity risk.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Graph coloring likely solved to 0 conflicts; TSP likely improved with a cleaner geometric tour; MWIS remains baseline unless a clearly valid weight-improving swap is found. One more turn would focus on validating/refining TSP or a small MWIS augmentation.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Prioritize graph coloring and TSP for likely quick gains over baseline; use MWIS only if enough time remains.
For graph coloring, repair baseline conflicts by targeted recoloring around conflicted nodes, aiming for 0 conflicts.
For TSP, improve baseline tour by geometric ordering and local edge-crossing removal, aiming materially below 532.
For MWIS, start from baseline independent set and try weight-improving swaps involving highest-weight excluded vertices (V121,V139,V086,V064,V046,V053,V140,V115,V067,V065).
Need final output as one JSON object with all three components valid.
After each subtask, compare expected portfolio gain versus time cost.
Prefer a clean feasible answer on all three over aggressive but risky changes.
If graph coloring reaches 0-conflict and TSP is clearly improved, stopping may be optimal.
Keep careful exact-weight accounting for MWIS validity.
